[3] PREAMBLE
[4] This Settlement Agreement and General Release of All Claims ("Agreement") is entered into as of May 5, 2025 ("Effective Date"), by and between:
[5] Marcus R. Delano, an individual residing at 1847 SE Hawthorne Boulevard, Portland, OR 97214 (hereinafter referred to as "Delano" or "Employee"); and
[6] Greenleaf Organic Foods, Inc., an Oregon corporation with its principal place of business at 4200 NW Yeon Avenue, Portland, OR 97210 (hereinafter referred to as "Greenleaf," "the Company," or "Employer").
[7] Delano and Greenleaf are collectively referred to herein as "the Parties" and individually as a "Party."
[8] RECITALS
[9] WHEREAS, Delano was employed by Greenleaf from June 12, 2017, through March 14, 2025, a period of approximately seven years and nine months, during which time Delano served in progressively responsible roles within the Company's operations division;
[10] WHEREAS, Delano was promoted to the position of Vice President of Supply Chain Operations effective January 1, 2020, and at the time of his separation from the Company, earned a base annual salary of Two Hundred Eighteen Thousand Dollars ($218,000.00), in addition to eligibility for performance-based incentive compensation and equity awards;
[11] WHEREAS, on November 8, 2024, Delano submitted a good-faith internal complaint to Greenleaf's Human Resources Director, Leanne Foss, reporting concerns regarding cold-chain protocol failures at the Company's Bend, Oregon distribution facility that Delano believed constituted potential violations of FDA food safety regulations, including but not limited to requirements under the Food Safety Modernization Act and applicable FDA guidance on temperature controls for perishable organic food products;
[12] WHEREAS, on December 3, 2024, Delano filed a complaint with Oregon Occupational Safety and Health Administration ("Oregon OSHA") (Complaint No. OR-OSHA-2024-11872) regarding the same cold-chain protocol failures and related food safety concerns at the Company's Bend, Oregon facility;
[13] WHEREAS, on January 22, 2025, Oregon OSHA opened a formal investigation of the Company's Bend, Oregon facility in connection with the matters raised in Complaint No. OR-OSHA-2024-11872, which investigation remains pending and unresolved as of the date of this Agreement;
[14] WHEREAS, on February 10, 2025, Greenleaf placed Delano on paid administrative leave, and on March 14, 2025, Greenleaf terminated Delano's employment, citing "elimination of position due to restructuring of supply chain division" as the basis for the termination;
[15] WHEREAS, Delano asserts claims against Greenleaf including, but not limited to, retaliation in violation of ORS 654.062(5) (Oregon whistleblower protection), age discrimination in violation of the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967 ("ADEA"), 29 U.S.C. § 621 et seq., and employment discrimination claims under Oregon's employment discrimination statutes, ORS Chapter 659A, as well as related common law claims;
[16] WHEREAS, Greenleaf denies any and all wrongdoing or liability whatsoever and contends that Delano's termination was the result of a legitimate business reorganization unrelated to any complaint or protected activity by Delano;
[17] WHEREAS, the Parties desire to fully and finally resolve all disputes, claims, and controversies between them, including all claims arising out of or related to Delano's employment with Greenleaf and the termination thereof, without the expense, delay, uncertainty, and burden of litigation;
[18] NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual promises, covenants, and agreements set forth herein, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged by each Party, the Parties agree as follows:
[19] 
[20] AGREEMENT
[21] Section 1. Definitions
[22] As used in this Agreement, the following terms shall have the meanings set forth below:
[23] (a) "Agreement" means this Settlement Agreement and General Release of All Claims, including all recitals, sections, and exhibits hereto.
[24] (b) "Effective Date" means May 5, 2025, the date first set forth above.
[25] (c) "Separation Date" means March 14, 2025, the date on which Delano's employment with Greenleaf was terminated.
[26] (d) "Releasees" means Greenleaf Organic Foods, Inc., and each of its past, present, and future officers, directors, shareholders, members, employees, agents, attorneys, representatives, successors, assigns, parent companies, subsidiaries, and affiliates, including without limitation Sandra Wojcik (Chief Executive Officer), Tyler Huang (General Counsel), Patricia Engel (Chief Operating Officer), and Leanne Foss (Human Resources Director), each in their individual and official capacities.
[27] (e) "Releasors" means Marcus R. Delano, individually and on behalf of his heirs, executors, administrators, successors, and assigns.
[28] (f) "RSU" means a Restricted Stock Unit as defined under the Greenleaf 2021 Equity Incentive Plan, as amended from time to time.
[29] (g) "Settlement Payment" means the aggregate payments and other consideration described in Section 3 of this Agreement.
[30] Section 2. Separation of Employment
[31] The Parties acknowledge and agree that Delano's employment with Greenleaf terminated effective March 14, 2025 (the "Separation Date"). Delano's last day physically present at Greenleaf's offices was February 10, 2025, the date on which Delano was placed on paid administrative leave. Delano confirms that he has received all wages, salary, and compensation earned through the Separation Date, including payment for all accrued but unused paid time off ("PTO"), and that no further wages, salary, commissions, bonuses, or benefits of any kind are owed to Delano by Greenleaf except as specifically provided in this Agreement. Greenleaf shall provide Delano with information regarding continuation of health insurance benefits under the Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act ("COBRA") within the timeframes required by applicable federal and state law. The Parties acknowledge that the terms of this Section 2 are not in dispute.
[32] Section 3. Settlement Consideration
[33] In consideration of the promises, covenants, releases, and agreements set forth herein, Greenleaf agrees to provide Delano with the following Settlement Payment:
[34] (a) Compensatory Damages Payment. Greenleaf shall pay to Delano the sum of Two Hundred Seventy-Five Thousand Dollars ($275,000.00) as compensatory damages for alleged emotional distress, reputational harm, and other non-wage losses sustained by Delano in connection with the claims resolved by this Agreement. This amount shall be treated as non-wage income and shall be reported on IRS Form 1099-MISC issued to Delano. This payment shall not be subject to federal, state, or local payroll tax withholding, and Delano shall be solely responsible for the payment of any taxes due on this amount. Payment shall be made within fourteen (14) calendar days of the Effective Date by wire transfer to an account designated by Delano in writing or, at Greenleaf's election, by company check delivered to Delano at the address set forth in the Preamble.
[35] (b) Back Pay. Greenleaf shall pay to Delano the sum of One Hundred Twenty-Five Thousand Dollars ($125,000.00) as back pay representing lost wages from the Separation Date through the Effective Date. This amount shall be treated as wage income and shall be subject to all applicable federal, state, and local payroll tax withholdings, including without limitation FICA (Social Security and Medicare), federal income tax withholding, and Oregon state income tax withholding. Greenleaf shall report this payment on IRS Form W-2 for the applicable tax year. Payment of the net amount (after all applicable withholdings) shall be made within fourteen (14) calendar days of the Effective Date by the same method described in Section 3(a).
[36] (c) Attorney Fees and Costs. Greenleaf shall pay the sum of Seventy-Five Thousand Dollars ($75,000.00) directly to Engel & Associates, P.C., 700 SW Taylor Street, Suite 400, Portland, OR 97205, as and for Delano's attorney fees and costs incurred in connection with the investigation, pursuit, and resolution of the claims resolved by this Agreement. This payment shall be reported on IRS Form 1099-NEC issued to Engel & Associates, P.C., at its federal Tax Identification Number. Payment shall be made within fourteen (14) calendar days of the Effective Date by wire transfer or company check delivered to Engel & Associates, P.C., at the address set forth above.
[37] (d) Accelerated Vesting of Restricted Stock Units. Greenleaf shall cause the immediate accelerated vesting of Five Thousand (5,000) Restricted Stock Units previously granted to Delano under the Greenleaf 2021 Equity Incentive Plan pursuant to the RSU Grant Agreement dated July 1, 2022 (the "RSU Grant"). The Parties agree that for purposes of this Agreement, the fair market value of each RSU is Ten Dollars ($10.00) per share, based on the most recent independent valuation conducted by Ridgepoint Valuation Services, representing a total value of Fifty Thousand Dollars ($50,000.00). The accelerated RSUs shall vest and be delivered to Delano, or to a brokerage account designated by Delano in writing, within thirty (30) calendar days of the Effective Date. Any tax obligations arising from the accelerated vesting and delivery of the RSUs shall be governed by the terms of the Greenleaf 2021 Equity Incentive Plan and applicable tax law.
[38] (e) Total Settlement Value. The aggregate Settlement Payment under this Agreement is Five Hundred Twenty-Five Thousand Dollars ($525,000.00), comprised of the compensatory damages payment ($275,000.00), the back pay payment ($125,000.00), the attorney fees payment ($75,000.00), and the RSU acceleration ($50,000.00).
[39] 
[40] Section 4. General Release of Claims by Delano
[41] In consideration of the Settlement Payment and other promises set forth in this Agreement, Delano, for himself and on behalf of each of the Releasors, hereby irrevocably and unconditionally releases, acquits, and forever discharges each of the Releasees from any and all claims, demands, actions, causes of action, suits, debts, dues, sums of money, accounts, reckonings, bonds, bills, covenants, contracts, controversies, agreements, promises, variances, trespasses, damages, judgments, extents, executions, losses, expenses, liabilities, and obligations of every kind and nature whatsoever, whether known or unknown, suspected or unsuspected, accrued or unaccrued, fixed or contingent, that Delano has, has ever had, or may hereafter have against any of the Releasees, arising out of, related to, or in any way connected with Delano's employment with Greenleaf, the terms and conditions of that employment, or the termination thereof, from the beginning of time through the Effective Date.
[42] Without limiting the generality of the foregoing release, Delano specifically releases and waives all claims arising under or related to any of the following federal, state, and local statutes, regulations, ordinances, and common law theories, as amended, to the fullest extent permitted by law:
[43] (i) Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, 42 U.S.C. § 2000e et seq.;
[44] (ii) the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967 ("ADEA"), 29 U.S.C. § 621 et seq.;
[45] (iii) the Americans with Disabilities Act ("ADA"), 42 U.S.C. § 12101 et seq.;
[46] (iv) the Family and Medical Leave Act ("FMLA"), 29 U.S.C. § 2601 et seq.;
[47] (v) the Employee Retirement Income Security Act ("ERISA"), 29 U.S.C. § 1001 et seq.;
[48] (vi) the Equal Pay Act, 29 U.S.C. § 206(d);
[49] (vii) the Genetic Information Nondiscrimination Act ("GINA"), 42 U.S.C. § 2000ff et seq.;
[50] (viii) 42 U.S.C. §§ 1981 and 1983;
[51] (ix) the Oregon Workplace Fairness Act and Oregon's employment discrimination statutes, ORS Chapter 659A;
[52] (x) Oregon whistleblower protection statutes, including ORS 654.062(5);
[53] (xi) Oregon wage and hour laws, including ORS Chapter 652 and ORS Chapter 653;
[54] (xii) workers' compensation claims under ORS Chapter 656;
[55] (xiii) any other federal, state, or local statute, regulation, ordinance, executive order, or constitutional provision relating to employment, employment discrimination, retaliation, wages, benefits, or conditions of employment; and
[56] (xiv) any and all claims arising under common law, including but not limited to breach of contract (express or implied), breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, tortious interference, defamation, slander, libel, fraud, fraudulent inducement, negligent misrepresentation, negligence, wrongful discharge, intentional infliction of emotional distress, negligent infliction of emotional distress, invasion of privacy, and promissory estoppel.
[57] Delano acknowledges that he may hereafter discover facts different from or in addition to those that he currently knows or believes to be true with respect to the claims released herein, and Delano expressly agrees that this release shall remain effective in all respects notwithstanding any such discovery. Delano hereby expressly waives any and all rights he may have under any statute or common law principle that would otherwise limit the scope of this release to those claims actually known or suspected to exist at the time of execution of this Agreement.
[58] Section 5. ADEA / Age Discrimination Specific Release
[59] Delano acknowledges that he is knowingly and voluntarily waiving and releasing any and all rights or claims he may have under the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967 ("ADEA"), as amended by the Older Workers Benefit Protection Act ("OWBPA"), 29 U.S.C. § 621 et seq. Delano further acknowledges and agrees as follows:
[60] (a) Delano has read this Agreement in its entirety, understands each of its terms and provisions, and agrees to be bound hereby.
[61] (b) The consideration provided to Delano under Section 3 of this Agreement exceeds anything of value to which Delano is already entitled and constitutes adequate and sufficient consideration for the release of ADEA claims contained herein.
[62] (c) Delano has been given a period of fourteen (14) calendar days from the date of receipt of this Agreement within which to consider whether to execute it, which Delano acknowledges is a sufficient and reasonable period of time.
[63] (d) Delano is entering into this Agreement of his own free will and volition, without coercion, duress, threat, or undue pressure from any person or entity, and with full knowledge of its terms, effects, and consequences.
[64] (e) Delano acknowledges that this Agreement is written in a manner calculated to be understood by him and that he, in fact, understands the terms, conditions, and effect of this Agreement.
[65] (f) Delano has not been coerced, threatened, or otherwise pressured into signing this Agreement, and Delano's decision to execute this Agreement has been made freely, knowingly, and voluntarily.
[66] 
[67] Section 6. Confidentiality
[68] Delano agrees that the terms, amount, and existence of this Agreement, including the Settlement Payment and each of its component parts, shall be kept strictly confidential. Delano shall not disclose, publish, or communicate any information regarding this Agreement, its terms, its financial components, or the negotiations leading to its execution to any person or entity, except as follows:
[69] (i) to Delano's spouse or domestic partner, provided that such individual agrees to maintain the confidentiality of the information;
[70] (ii) to Delano's legal counsel at Engel & Associates, P.C., or any successor legal counsel retained by Delano;
[71] (iii) to Delano's tax advisor, accountant, or financial advisor, solely for the purpose of obtaining tax, accounting, or financial advice, provided that such advisor agrees to maintain the confidentiality of the information; or
[72] (iv) as required by applicable law, regulation, court order, subpoena, or other compulsory legal process.
[73] In the event that Delano is compelled by legal process to disclose any information subject to this confidentiality provision, Delano shall provide Greenleaf with prompt written notice of such compulsion so as to permit Greenleaf to seek a protective order or other appropriate relief. In the event that Delano breaches this confidentiality provision, Greenleaf shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief, specific performance, and all other available legal and equitable remedies, including recovery of damages sustained as a result of such breach.
[74] Section 7. Non-Disparagement
[75] Neither Party shall, at any time after the Effective Date, make, publish, or communicate, or cause or encourage any other person or entity to make, publish, or communicate, any disparaging, defamatory, derogatory, or negative statements, whether written or oral, about the other Party, including but not limited to statements regarding the other Party's business practices, products, services, financial condition, management, employees, corporate governance, or the circumstances of Delano's employment with or separation from Greenleaf. For purposes of this Section 7, "Greenleaf" includes its officers, directors, and members of senior management, specifically including Sandra Wojcik (CEO), Tyler Huang (General Counsel), Patricia Engel (COO), and Leanne Foss (HR Director), each in their individual and official capacities. Any breach of this non-disparagement provision shall entitle the non-breaching Party to seek injunctive relief and all other available legal and equitable remedies. The obligations set forth in this Section 7 shall survive the expiration or termination of this Agreement and shall continue in perpetuity.
[76] Section 8. Tax Provisions
[77] The Parties acknowledge and agree that the tax treatment of the Settlement Payment shall be as follows:
[78] (a) The compensatory damages payment of $275,000.00 described in Section 3(a) shall be treated as non-wage income, shall be reported on IRS Form 1099-MISC issued to Delano, and shall not be subject to federal, state, or local payroll tax withholding. Delano shall be solely responsible for the timely payment of all federal, state, and local income taxes due on this amount.
[79] (b) The back pay payment of $125,000.00 described in Section 3(b) shall be treated as wage income, shall be subject to all applicable federal, state, and local payroll tax withholdings (including FICA, federal income tax, and Oregon state income tax), and shall be reported on IRS Form W-2 for the applicable tax year.
[80] (c) The attorney fees payment of $75,000.00 described in Section 3(c) shall be reported on IRS Form 1099-NEC issued to Engel & Associates, P.C., at its federal Tax Identification Number.
[81] (d) The accelerated vesting of Restricted Stock Units described in Section 3(d) shall be treated in accordance with the terms of the Greenleaf 2021 Equity Incentive Plan and applicable provisions of the Internal Revenue Code.
[82] Greenleaf makes no representations or warranties to Delano regarding the tax consequences of any payment or benefit provided under this Agreement, and Delano acknowledges that he has not relied upon any statement or representation by Greenleaf or its attorneys concerning the tax treatment of any portion of the Settlement Payment. Greenleaf shall have no obligation to indemnify, defend, or hold harmless Delano for any tax liability, including interest and penalties, arising from or related to the payments or benefits made under this Agreement. Each Party shall be responsible for its own tax obligations arising from the transactions contemplated by this Agreement.
[83] 
[84] Section 9. Non-Competition
[85] For a period of eighteen (18) months following the Separation Date, Delano shall not, directly or indirectly, whether as an employee, consultant, independent contractor, officer, director, partner, member, owner, investor (other than as a holder of less than two percent (2%) of the outstanding shares of a publicly traded company), agent, advisor, or in any other capacity, engage in, be employed by, consult for, or otherwise provide services to any competitor in the organic food industry within the states of Oregon, Washington, Idaho, and California.
[86] For purposes of this Section 9, a "competitor" shall mean any business, firm, company, corporation, partnership, limited liability company, or other entity that manufactures, distributes, markets, sells, or otherwise deals in organic food products, including but not limited to granola, trail mixes, dried fruit, frozen meals, plant-based snacks, organic beverages, or similar products.
[87] In the event that Delano breaches this non-competition provision, Greenleaf shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief, specific performance, and all other available legal and equitable remedies, including recovery of damages sustained as a result of such breach. In addition, the eighteen (18) month non-competition period shall be tolled during any period during which Delano is in breach of this provision, and shall resume upon the date on which such breach ceases.
[88] Section 10. Non-Solicitation
[89] For a period of eighteen (18) months following the Separation Date, Delano shall not, directly or indirectly, whether on his own behalf or on behalf of any other person or entity:
[90] (a) solicit, recruit, hire, engage, or encourage, or attempt to solicit, recruit, hire, engage, or encourage, any employee, independent contractor, or consultant of Greenleaf to leave the Company's employ or engagement, or to accept employment or engagement with any other person or entity;
[91] (b) solicit, divert, or take away, or attempt to solicit, divert, or take away, the business or patronage of any customer, supplier, vendor, distributor, or business partner of Greenleaf with whom Delano had material contact or about whom Delano obtained confidential information during the last two (2) years of his employment with Greenleaf.
[92] For the avoidance of doubt, this non-solicitation provision shall not prohibit Delano from making general solicitations of employment through public advertisements or employment postings not specifically directed at Greenleaf employees. In the event of a breach, Greenleaf shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief and all other available legal and equitable remedies.
[93] Section 11. Employment References
[94] Upon request from any prospective employer or other third party, Greenleaf agrees to provide a positive, neutral, or at minimum non-negative employment reference for Delano. Such reference shall confirm Delano's dates of employment (June 12, 2017, through March 14, 2025), his final title of Vice President of Supply Chain Operations, and shall include a statement that Delano was a valued member of the Greenleaf team who made significant contributions to the Company's supply chain operations during his tenure. All reference inquiries regarding Delano shall be directed to Leanne Foss, Human Resources Director, or her designee. Greenleaf shall instruct Sandra Wojcik, Tyler Huang, Patricia Engel, Leanne Foss, and all other current employees who may receive reference inquiries concerning Delano to refrain from providing any information beyond the reference described above and shall not, under any circumstances, provide any information suggesting that Delano was terminated for cause, for performance-related reasons, or in connection with any internal complaint or investigation. Greenleaf shall issue written instructions to the foregoing individuals within seven (7) calendar days of the Effective Date implementing the requirements of this Section 11.
[95] Section 12. Mutual Release by Greenleaf
[96] Greenleaf, on behalf of itself and each of the Releasees, hereby irrevocably and unconditionally releases, acquits, and forever discharges Delano from any and all claims, demands, actions, causes of action, suits, damages, losses, expenses, and liabilities of every kind and nature, whether known or unknown, suspected or unsuspected, that Greenleaf has, has ever had, or may hereafter have against Delano, arising out of, related to, or in any way connected with Delano's employment with Greenleaf or the termination thereof, from the beginning of time through the Effective Date, except for claims arising from: (i) Delano's fraud, embezzlement, or criminal conduct discovered after the Effective Date; (ii) Delano's breach of any provision of this Agreement; or (iii) any obligation of Delano that survives termination of employment under any prior written agreement between the Parties, to the extent that such obligation is not superseded by this Agreement.
[97] 
[98] Section 13. No Admission of Liability
[99] This Agreement is a compromise and settlement of disputed claims. Nothing contained in this Agreement, including the payment of the Settlement Payment, shall be construed as an admission of liability, wrongdoing, or violation of any federal, state, or local law, regulation, or ordinance by either Party. Greenleaf specifically denies that it engaged in any unlawful retaliation, age discrimination, or other wrongful or discriminatory conduct toward Delano or any other individual. Delano specifically denies any wrongdoing or misconduct in connection with his employment with Greenleaf. The Parties enter into this Agreement solely for the purpose of avoiding the costs, burdens, and uncertainties of litigation and to achieve a full and final resolution of all disputes between them.
[100] Section 14. Re-Employment Eligibility
[101] Delano shall not be barred from future employment with Greenleaf and shall be considered for open positions for which he is qualified, on the same basis as any other external applicant, without prejudice or adverse consideration arising from the circumstances of his separation from employment or the claims asserted in connection with this Agreement. Greenleaf shall not maintain any internal notation, flag, code, or designation in its human resources records, personnel files, applicant tracking systems, or any other employment-related database or record that would indicate Delano is ineligible for rehire or that would otherwise adversely affect Delano's candidacy for future employment with Greenleaf.
[102] Section 15. Representations and Warranties
[103] (a) Delano represents and warrants that:
[104] (i) He has not assigned, transferred, conveyed, or otherwise disposed of any claim, demand, right, or cause of action released herein to any person or entity;
[105] (ii) He has not filed any lawsuit, complaint, charge, or other proceeding against Greenleaf or any of the Releasees with any court, tribunal, or governmental agency, other than the Oregon OSHA complaint referenced in the Recitals above (Complaint No. OR-OSHA-2024-11872), and he agrees not to file any such lawsuit, complaint, charge, or proceeding in the future with respect to any claim released herein, except as may be required by law or to enforce the terms of this Agreement;
[106] (iii) He has the full legal capacity and authority to execute this Agreement and to perform his obligations hereunder, and his execution and performance of this Agreement does not conflict with or violate any other agreement to which he is a party.
[107] (b) Greenleaf represents and warrants that:
[108] (i) It has the full corporate power and authority to enter into this Agreement and to perform its obligations hereunder, and the execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement has been duly authorized by all necessary corporate action;
[109] (ii) The person executing this Agreement on behalf of Greenleaf is duly authorized to do so and to bind Greenleaf to the terms and conditions contained herein.
[110] Section 16. Execution and Consideration Period
[111] Delano shall have fourteen (14) calendar days from the date of receipt of this Agreement to review, consider, and execute it. If Delano does not execute and return a signed copy of this Agreement to Engel & Associates, P.C., or to Greenleaf's General Counsel within the fourteen (14) calendar day period, this offer of settlement shall automatically expire and be of no further force or effect, and neither Party shall have any obligation hereunder.
[112] This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which, when taken together, shall constitute one and the same instrument. Facsimile signatures and electronic (PDF) signatures transmitted by email or other electronic means shall be deemed original signatures for all purposes of this Agreement and shall be sufficient to bind each Party hereto.
[113] 
[114] Section 17. Severability
[115] If any provision of this Agreement, or the application thereof to any person, entity, or circumstance, is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, such invalidity, illegality, or unenforceability shall not affect any other provision of this Agreement, and the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect as if such invalid, illegal, or unenforceable provision had not been included herein. The Parties agree that in the event any provision is determined to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, such provision shall be modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid, legal, and enforceable while preserving the Parties' original intent to the greatest extent possible.
[116] Section 18. Entire Agreement
[117] This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous negotiations, representations, agreements, promises, and understandings, whether written or oral, relating to such subject matter, including but not limited to any prior offer letters, employment agreements, memoranda of understanding, or term sheets exchanged between the Parties in connection with the resolution of the disputes addressed herein. No amendment, modification, supplement, or waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless made in writing and signed by both Parties. No waiver of any breach or default under this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of any subsequent breach or default.
[118] IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Settlement Agreement and General Release of All Claims as of the date first set forth above.

        ind=pPr.find(f'{W}ind')
        if ind is not None:
            desc.append('ind:left='+str(ind.get(f'{W}left',''))+',hanging='+str(ind.get(f'{W}hanging','')))
        numPr=pPr.find(f'{W}numPr')
        if numPr is not None:
            il=numPr.find(f'{W}ilvl'); ni=numPr.find(f'{W}numId')
            desc.append('numPr:ilvl='+str(il.get(f'{W}val') if il is not None else '')+',numId='+str(ni.get(f'{W}val') if ni is not None else ''))
        jc=pPr.find(f'{W}jc')
        if jc is not None: desc.append('jc='+jc.get(f'{W}val'))
        sp=pPr.find(f'{W}spacing')
        if sp is not None: desc.append('spacing:before='+str(sp.get(f'{W}before',''))+',after='+str(sp.get(f'{W}after','')))
    runs=p.findall(f'{W}r')
    bold = any((r.find(f'{W}rPr') is not None and r.find(f'{W}rPr').find(f'{W}b') is not None) for r in runs)
    if bold: desc.append('BOLD')
    return ' '.join(desc) if desc else 'none'
for idx in [21,22,23,30,32,33,34,40,42,43,58,59,60,67,68,84,88,93,100,102,110,114,116,118]:
    p=paras[idx]
    runs=p.findall(f'{W}r')
    text=''.join((r.find(f'{W}t').text or '') for r in runs if r.find(f'{W}t') is not None)
    print(f'[{idx}] props={describe(p)!r}')
    print(f'     text={text[:70]!r}')
""}

{"command": "cd $WORKSPACE_DIR && python3 -c "
from lxml import etree
W='{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/wordprocessingml/2006/main}'
tree = etree.parse('work/orig/word/document.xml')
root = tree.getroot()
body = root.find(f'{W}body')
paras = body.findall(f'{W}p')
def describe(p):
    pPr=p.find(f'{W}pPr')
    desc=[]
    if pPr is not None:
        ind=pPr.find(f'{W}ind')
        if ind is not None:
            desc.append('ind:left='+str(ind.get(f'{W}left',''))+',hanging='+str(ind.get(f'{W}hanging','')))
        numPr=pPr.find(f'{W}numPr')
        if numPr is not None:
            il=numPr.find(f'{W}ilvl'); ni=numPr.find(f'{W}numId')
            desc.append('numPr:ilvl='+str(il.get(f'{W}val') if il is not None else '')+',numId='+str(ni.get(f'{W}val') if ni is not None else ''))
        jc=pPr.find(f'{W}jc')
        if jc is not None: desc.append('jc='+jc.get(f'{W}val'))
        sp=pPr.find(f'{W}spacing')
        if sp is not None: desc.append('spacing:before='+str(sp.get(f'{W}before',''))+',after='+str(sp.get(f'{W}after','')))
    runs=p.findall(f'{W}r')
    bold = any((r.find(f'{W}rPr') is not None and r.find(f'{W}rPr').find(f'{W}b') is not None) for r in runs)
    if bold: desc.append('BOLD')
    return ' '.join(desc) if desc else 'none'
for idx in [21,22,23,30,32,33,34,40,42,43,58,59,60,67,68,84,88,93,100,102,110,114,116,118]:
    p=paras[idx]
    runs=p.findall(f'{W}r')
    text=''.join((r.find(f'{W}t').text or '') for r in runs if r.find(f'{W}t') is not None)
    print(f'[{idx}] props={describe(p)!r}')
    print(f'     text={text[:70]!r}')
""}

import zipfile
with zipfile.ZipFile('documents/draft-settlement-agreement.docx') as z:
    data = z.read('word/document.xml').decode('utf-8')
# Check for smart quotes vs straight
print('Has smart left double (\u201c):', '\u201c' in data)
print('Has smart right double (\u201d):', '\u201d' in data)
print('Has smart right single/apos (\u2019):', '\u2019' in data)
print('Has straight double (\"):', chr(34) in data)
print('Has straight apos (\u0027):', chr(39) in data)

print('Has section sign (\u00a7):', '\u00a7' in data)
# Show a snippet around 'Agreement'
i = data.find('Settlement Agreement and General Release of All Claims')
print(repr(data[i:i+90]))
""}

{"file_path": "work/redline_custom.py", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Custom tracked-changes redline for the Delano settlement agreement.

Reads the original draft-settlement-agreement.docx, applies a set of
replacements (word-level diff -> w:ins/w:del, preserving run formatting)
and insertions (fully-inserted paragraphs), and writes a new .docx with
native Word tracked changes. Unchanged paragraphs are copied verbatim so
all original formatting is preserved.
"""
import copy
import zipfile
from pathlib import Path
from lxml import etree
from diff_match_patch import diff_match_patch

W = "http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/wordprocessingml/2006/main"
XML = "http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace"
WF = f"{{{W}}}"

AUTHOR = "Greenleaf GC Review"
WHEN = "2025-05-12T00:00:00Z"

ORIG = Path("documents/draft-settlement-agreement.docx")
OUT = Path("work/markup_intermediate.docx")

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Edit data
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Replacements: paragraph index -> new full paragraph text.
# (Paragraph indices match the original document order, 0-based, excluding sectPr.)
REPLACEMENTS = {
    4: 'This Settlement Agreement and General Release of All Claims ("Agreement") is entered into as of the Effective Date (as defined in Section 1(b)), by and between:',
    24: '(b) "Effective Date" means the date on which this Agreement becomes effective and enforceable, which shall be the eighth (8th) calendar day after the date on which the last Party signs this Agreement, provided that Delano has not revoked this Agreement during the seven (7) calendar day Revocation Period set forth in Section 5(g) and Section 16. If Delano revokes this Agreement within the Revocation Period, this Agreement shall be null and void and there shall be no Effective Date.',
    31: 'The Parties acknowledge and agree that Delano\'s employment with Greenleaf terminated effective March 14, 2025 (the "Separation Date"). Delano\'s last day physically present at Greenleaf\'s offices was February 10, 2025, the date on which Delano was placed on paid administrative leave. Delano acknowledges that, except for the payments and benefits expressly provided in Section 3 of this Agreement (which, by mutual agreement of the Parties, include payment for any unpaid 2024 performance incentive compensation and for all accrued but unused paid time off ("PTO")), he has received all wages, salary, and compensation earned through the Separation Date, and that no further wages, salary, commissions, bonuses, or benefits of any kind are owed to Delano by Greenleaf other than as specifically provided in this Agreement. Greenleaf shall provide Delano with information regarding continuation of health insurance benefits under the Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act ("COBRA") within the timeframes required by applicable federal and state law.',
    35: '(b) Back Pay. Greenleaf shall pay to Delano the sum of One Hundred Twenty-Five Thousand Dollars ($125,000.00) as back pay representing lost wages and unpaid compensation (including any unpaid 2024 performance incentive compensation) from the Separation Date through the Effective Date. This amount shall be treated as wage income and shall be subject to all applicable federal, state, and local payroll tax withholdings, including without limitation FICA (Social Security and Medicare), federal income tax withholding, and Oregon state income tax withholding. Greenleaf shall report this payment on IRS Form W-2 for the applicable tax year. Payment of the net amount (after all applicable withholdings) shall be made within fourteen (14) calendar days of the Effective Date by the same method described in Section 3(a).',
    37: '(d) Accelerated Vesting of Restricted Stock Units. Greenleaf shall cause the accelerated vesting of Five Thousand (5,000) Restricted Stock Units previously granted to Delano under the Greenleaf 2021 Equity Incentive Plan pursuant to the RSU Grant Agreement dated July 1, 2022 (the "RSU Grant"). The Parties agree that for purposes of this Agreement, the fair market value of each RSU is Twelve Dollars and Fifty Cents ($12.50) per share, based on the most recent independent 409A valuation conducted by Ridgepoint Valuation Services dated January 15, 2025, representing a total value of Sixty-Two Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($62,500.00). The accelerated vesting contemplated by this Section 3(d) is subject to approval by the Compensation Committee of Greenleaf\'s Board of Directors, which has discretionary authority to approve accelerated vesting in connection with separation agreements under the Greenleaf 2021 Equity Incentive Plan. Greenleaf shall seek such approval promptly following the Effective Date. In the event the Compensation Committee does not approve the accelerated vesting within thirty (30) calendar days of the Effective Date, Greenleaf shall, at Delano\'s election, pay Delano a lump-sum cash payment of Sixty-Two Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($62,500.00) in lieu of the RSU acceleration, which cash payment shall be treated as non-wage income and reported on IRS Form 1099-MISC. The accelerated RSUs (or, if applicable, the cash payment) shall vest and be delivered to Delano, or to a brokerage account designated by Delano in writing, within thirty (30) calendar days of the Effective Date or, in the case of the cash payment, within fourteen (14) calendar days of Delano\'s election. Any tax obligations arising from the accelerated vesting and delivery of the RSUs shall be governed by the terms of the Greenleaf 2021 Equity Incentive Plan and applicable tax law.',
    38: '(e) Total Settlement Value. The aggregate Settlement Payment under this Agreement is Five Hundred Thirty-Seven Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($537,500.00), comprised of the compensatory damages payment ($275,000.00), the back pay payment ($125,000.00), the attorney fees payment ($75,000.00), and the RSU acceleration ($62,500.00).',
    62: '(c) Delano has been given a period of twenty-one (21) calendar days from the date of receipt of this Agreement within which to consider whether to execute it, which Delano acknowledges is a sufficient and reasonable period of time, and Delano acknowledges that he may use as much or as little of this period as he wishes prior to execution.',
    72: '(iv) as required by applicable law, regulation, court order, subpoena, or other compulsory legal process; or (v) to any federal, state, or local government agency, including but not limited to the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, the Occupational Safety and Health Administration, the National Labor Relations Board, the Securities and Exchange Commission, and the Oregon Bureau of Labor and Industries; provided, however, that nothing in this Section 6 shall prohibit Delano from communicating with, filing a charge or complaint with, or participating or cooperating in an investigation or proceeding conducted by, any such agency, and nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to prohibit Delano from receiving any monetary award or compensation from any government-administered whistleblower, tip, or award program.',
    75: 'Neither Party shall, at any time after the Effective Date, make, publish, or communicate, or cause or encourage any other person or entity to make, publish, or communicate, any disparaging, defamatory, derogatory, or negative statements, whether written or oral, about the other Party, including but not limited to statements regarding the other Party\'s business practices, products, services, financial condition, management, employees, corporate governance, or the circumstances of Delano\'s employment with or separation from Greenleaf. For purposes of this Section 7, "Greenleaf" includes its officers, directors, and members of senior management, specifically including Sandra Wojcik (CEO), Tyler Huang (General Counsel), Patricia Engel (COO), and Leanne Foss (HR Director), each in their individual and official capacities. Nothing in this Section 7 shall prohibit either Party from making truthful statements in response to a subpoena or other compulsory legal process, or from communicating with, filing a charge or complaint with, or participating or cooperating in an investigation or proceeding conducted by, any federal, state, or local government agency, or from making any truthful statement required by applicable law. Any breach of this non-disparagement provision shall entitle the non-breaching Party to seek injunctive relief and all other available legal and equitable remedies. The obligations set forth in this Section 7 shall survive the expiration or termination of this Agreement and shall continue for a period of three (3) years following the Effective Date.',
    85: 'For a period of eighteen (18) months following the Separation Date, Delano shall not, directly or indirectly, whether as an employee, consultant, independent contractor, officer, director, partner, member, owner, investor (other than as a holder of less than two percent (2%) of the outstanding shares of a publicly traded company), agent, advisor, or in any other capacity, engage in, be employed by, consult for, or otherwise provide services to any competitor in the organic food industry within the states of Oregon, Washington, and Idaho. The Parties acknowledge that this non-competition covenant is entered into in connection with the settlement of disputed claims and is supported by the consideration set forth in Section 3, and that Delano\'s annual gross salary and commissions at the time of termination exceeded the threshold prescribed by ORS 653.295. The Parties further acknowledge that the duration of this covenant does not exceed the eighteen (18) month maximum permitted by ORS 653.295. This Section 9 shall not apply within the State of California, where it is the Parties\' intent that no non-competition restriction shall be enforced to the extent prohibited by California Business and Professions Code Section 16600.',
    94: 'Upon request from any prospective employer or other third party, Greenleaf agrees to provide a neutral, factual employment reference for Delano. Such reference shall confirm Delano\'s dates of employment (June 12, 2017, through March 14, 2025) and his final title of Vice President of Supply Chain Operations, and shall state that Delano was employed in good standing and that his position was eliminated as part of a reorganization. All reference inquiries regarding Delano shall be directed to Leanne Foss, Human Resources Director, or her designee. Greenleaf shall instruct Sandra Wojcik, Tyler Huang, Patricia Engel, Leanne Foss, and all other current employees who may receive reference inquiries concerning Delano to refrain from providing any information beyond the reference described above and shall not, under any circumstances, provide any information suggesting that Delano was terminated for cause, for performance-related reasons, or in connection with any internal complaint or investigation. Greenleaf shall issue written instructions to the foregoing individuals within seven (7) calendar days of the Effective Date implementing the requirements of this Section 11.',
    105: '(ii) He has not filed any lawsuit, complaint, charge, or other proceeding against Greenleaf or any of the Releasees with any court, tribunal, or governmental agency, other than the Oregon OSHA complaint referenced in the Recitals above (Complaint No. OR-OSHA-2024-11872), and he agrees not to file any such lawsuit, complaint, charge, or proceeding in the future with respect to any claim released herein, except as may be required by law, to enforce the terms of this Agreement, or as expressly permitted by the government-agency cooperation and reporting provisions of Section 4, Section 6, and Section 7;',
    111: 'Delano shall have twenty-one (21) calendar days from the date of receipt of this Agreement to review, consider, and execute it. If Delano does not execute and return a signed copy of this Agreement to Engel & Associates, P.C., or to Greenleaf\'s General Counsel within the twenty-one (21) calendar day period, this offer of settlement shall automatically expire and be of no further force or effect, and neither Party shall have any obligation hereunder. Following Delano\'s execution of this Agreement, Delano shall have seven (7) calendar days to revoke this Agreement as provided in Section 5(g). This Agreement shall not become effective or enforceable, and no payment or benefit shall be owed, until the expiration of the seven (7) calendar day revocation period without revocation.',
    117: 'This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous negotiations, representations, agreements, promises, and understandings, whether written or oral, relating to such subject matter, including but not limited to any prior offer letters, employment agreements, memoranda of understanding, or term sheets exchanged between the Parties in connection with the resolution of the disputes addressed herein; provided, however, that this Agreement shall not supersede or modify the terms of the Greenleaf 2021 Equity Incentive Plan, the RSU Grant Agreement dated July 1, 2022, or any prior confidentiality, invention assignment, or restrictive covenant agreement between the Parties, each of which shall remain in full force and effect in accordance with its terms except as expressly modified by this Agreement. No amendment, modification, supplement, or waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless made in writing and signed by both Parties. No waiver of any breach or default under this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of any subsequent breach or default.',
}

# Insertions: list of (after_para_index, [ (text, kind), ... ])
# kind: 'section' (bold header), 'body' (justified body), 'list' (indented subsection)

Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to prohibit Delano from communicating with any such agency regarding possible violations of law, and Delano is not required to notify Greenleaf of any such communication. This Agreement does not release, waive, or impair any right Delano may have to receive a monetary award, bounty, or compensation from any government-administered whistleblower, tip, or award program, including under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 or the Consumer Financial Protection Act of 2010. The Parties acknowledge that the pending Oregon OSHA investigation (Complaint No. OR-OSHA-2024-11872) is a government proceeding that is not affected by this Agreement and that Delano may cooperate with that investigation.',

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_rev_id = [1000]

def next_id():
    _rev_id[0] += 1
    return _rev_id[0]


def rpr_key(rpr):
    if rpr is None:
        return "none"
    return etree.tostring(rpr, method="c14n").decode()


def make_run(text, rpr):
    r = etree.Element(f"{WF}r")
    if rpr is not None:
        r.append(copy.deepcopy(rpr))
    t = etree.SubElement(r, f"{WF}t")
    t.set(f"{{{XML}}}space", "preserve")
    t.text = text
    return r


def make_ins(text, rpr):
    ins = etree.Element(f"{WF}ins")
    ins.set(f"{WF}id", str(next_id()))
    ins.set(f"{WF}author", AUTHOR)
    ins.set(f"{WF}date", WHEN)
    ins.append(make_run(text, rpr))
    return ins


def make_del(text, rpr):
    d = etree.Element(f"{WF}del")
    d.set(f"{WF}id", str(next_id()))
    d.set(f"{WF}author", AUTHOR)
    d.set(f"{WF}date", WHEN)
    r = etree.SubElement(d, f"{WF}r")
    if rpr is not None:
        r.append(copy.deepcopy(rpr))
    t = etree.SubElement(r, f"{WF}delText")
    t.set(f"{{{XML}}}space", "preserve")
    t.text = text
    return d


def para_text_and_rprmap(p):
    """Return (full_text, rpr_list) where rpr_list[i] is the rPr element for char i."""
    full = []
    rprmap = []
    for r in p.findall(f"{WF}r"):
        rpr = r.find(f"{WF}rPr")
        t = r.find(f"{WF}t")
        if t is None or t.text is None:
            continue
        for ch in t.text:
            full.append(ch)
            rprmap.append(rpr)
    return "".join(full), rprmap


def diff_paragraph(orig_p, new_text):
    """Build a new <w:p> with tracked changes preserving pPr and run formatting."""
    pPr = orig_p.find(f"{WF}pPr")
    orig_text, rprmap = para_text_and_rprmap(orig_p)

    dmp = diff_match_patch()
    diffs = dmp.diff_main(orig_text, new_text)
    dmp.diff_cleanupSemantic(diffs)

    new_p = etree.Element(f"{WF}p")
    if pPr is not None:
        new_p.append(copy.deepcopy(pPr))

    # Walk diffs, building (op, text, rpr) segments
    segments = []  # (op, text, rpr)
    orig_pos = 0
    for op, text in diffs:
        if op == 0:  # equal
            for ch in text:
                rpr = rprmap[orig_pos] if orig_pos < len(rprmap) else (rprmap[-1] if rprmap else None)
                segments.append(("eq", ch, rpr))
                orig_pos += 1
        elif op == -1:  # delete
            for ch in text:
                rpr = rprmap[orig_pos] if orig_pos < len(rprmap) else (rprmap[-1] if rprmap else None)
                segments.append(("del", ch, rpr))
                orig_pos += 1
        else:  # insert
            # use rPr of preceding original char, else first run rPr
            rpr = rprmap[orig_pos - 1] if orig_pos > 0 and rprmap else (rprmap[0] if rprmap else None)
            for ch in text:
                segments.append(("ins", ch, rpr))

    # Group consecutive segments with same (op, rpr_key)
    grouped = []
    for op, ch, rpr in segments:
        key = (op, rpr_key(rpr))
        if grouped and grouped[-1][0] == key:
            grouped[-1][1] += ch
        else:
            grouped.append([key, ch, rpr])

    for (op, _rk), text, rpr in grouped:
        if not text:
            continue
        if op == "eq":
            new_p.append(make_run(text, rpr))
        elif op == "ins":
            new_p.append(make_ins(text, rpr))
        elif op == "del":
            new_p.append(make_del(text, rpr))

    return new_p


def make_inserted_paragraph(text, pPr_template, rpr_template):
    """Fully-inserted paragraph: paragraph mark + run marked as inserted."""
    new_p = etree.Element(f"{WF}p")
    pPr = copy.deepcopy(pPr_template)
    # mark paragraph mark as inserted
    rPr_in_pPr = pPr.find(f"{WF}rPr")
    if rPr_in_pPr is None:
        rPr_in_pPr = etree.SubElement(pPr, f"{WF}rPr")
    ins_mark = etree.SubElement(rPr_in_pPr, f"{WF}ins")
    ins_mark.set(f"{WF}id", str(next_id()))
    ins_mark.set(f"{WF}author", AUTHOR)
    ins_mark.set(f"{WF}date", WHEN)
    new_p.append(pPr)
    # inserted run
    ins = etree.Element(f"{WF}ins")
    ins.set(f"{WF}id", str(next_id()))
    ins.set(f"{WF}author", AUTHOR)
    ins.set(f"{WF}date", WHEN)
    ins.append(make_run(text, rpr_template))
    new_p.append(ins)
    return new_p


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def main():
    with zipfile.ZipFile(ORIG) as z:
        names = z.namelist()
        doc_xml = z.read("word/document.xml")

    root = etree.fromstring(doc_xml)
    body = root.find(f"{WF}body")
    children = list(body)
    # children: 134 paragraphs (index 0..133) + sectPr (index 134)
    paragraphs = [c for c in children if c.tag == f"{WF}p"]
    sectPr = body.find(f"{WF}sectPr")

    # Template pPr / rPr
    pPr_section = paragraphs[21].find(f"{WF}pPr")
    pPr_body = paragraphs[22].find(f"{WF}pPr")
    pPr_list = paragraphs[43].find(f"{WF}pPr")
    rpr_section = paragraphs[21].findall(f"{WF}r")[0].find(f"{WF}rPr")
    rpr_body = paragraphs[22].findall(f"{WF}r")[0].find(f"{WF}rPr")

    # Build insertion map: after_index -> list of (text, kind)
    ins_map = {}
    for after_idx, items in INSERTIONS:
        ins_map.setdefault(after_idx, []).extend(items)

    # Rebuild body
    new_children = []
    for idx, p in enumerate(paragraphs):
        if idx in REPLACEMENTS:
            new_children.append(diff_paragraph(p, REPLACEMENTS[idx]))
        else:
            new_children.append(p)  # verbatim, full formatting preserved
        if idx in ins_map:
            for text, kind in ins_map[idx]:
                if kind == "section":
                    new_children.append(make_inserted_paragraph(text, pPr_section, rpr_section))
                elif kind == "list":
                    new_children.append(make_inserted_paragraph(text, pPr_list, rpr_body))
                else:
                    new_children.append(make_inserted_paragraph(text, pPr_body, rpr_body))

    # Clear body and re-append
    for c in list(body):
        body.remove(c)
    for c in new_children:
        body.append(c)
    if sectPr is not None:
        body.append(sectPr)

    new_doc_xml = etree.tostring(root, xml_declaration=True, encoding="UTF-8", standalone=True)

    OUT.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    with zipfile.ZipFile(ORIG) as zin, zipfile.ZipFile(OUT, "w", zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as zout:
        for item in zin.infolist():
            if item.filename == "word/document.xml":
                zout.writestr(item, new_doc_xml)
            else:
                zout.writestr(item, zin.read(item.filename))

    print(f"OK: wrote {OUT}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()"}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_rev_id = [1000]

def next_id():
    _rev_id[0] += 1
    return _rev_id[0]


def rpr_key(rpr):
    if rpr is None:
        return "none"
    return etree.tostring(rpr, method="c14n").decode()


def make_run(text, rpr):
    r = etree.Element(f"{WF}r")
    if rpr is not None:
        r.append(copy.deepcopy(rpr))
    t = etree.SubElement(r, f"{WF}t")
    t.set(f"{{{XML}}}space", "preserve")
    t.text = text
    return r


def make_ins(text, rpr):
    ins = etree.Element(f"{WF}ins")
    ins.set(f"{WF}id", str(next_id()))
    ins.set(f"{WF}author", AUTHOR)
    ins.set(f"{WF}date", WHEN)
    ins.append(make_run(text, rpr))
    return ins


def make_del(text, rpr):
    d = etree.Element(f"{WF}del")
    d.set(f"{WF}id", str(next_id()))
    d.set(f"{WF}author", AUTHOR)
    d.set(f"{WF}date", WHEN)
    r = etree.SubElement(d, f"{WF}r")
    if rpr is not None:
        r.append(copy.deepcopy(rpr))
    t = etree.SubElement(r, f"{WF}delText")
    t.set(f"{{{XML}}}space", "preserve")
    t.text = text
    return d


def para_text_and_rprmap(p):
    """Return (full_text, rpr_list) where rpr_list[i] is the rPr element for char i."""
    full = []
    rprmap = []
    for r in p.findall(f"{WF}r"):
        rpr = r.find(f"{WF}rPr")
        t = r.find(f"{WF}t")
        if t is None or t.text is None:
            continue
        for ch in t.text:
            full.append(ch)
            rprmap.append(rpr)
    return "".join(full), rprmap


def diff_paragraph(orig_p, new_text):
    """Build a new <w:p> with tracked changes preserving pPr and run formatting."""
    pPr = orig_p.find(f"{WF}pPr")
    orig_text, rprmap = para_text_and_rprmap(orig_p)

    dmp = diff_match_patch()
    diffs = dmp.diff_main(orig_text, new_text)
    dmp.diff_cleanupSemantic(diffs)

    new_p = etree.Element(f"{WF}p")
    if pPr is not None:
        new_p.append(copy.deepcopy(pPr))

    # Walk diffs, building (op, text, rpr) segments
    segments = []  # (op, text, rpr)
    orig_pos = 0
    for op, text in diffs:
        if op == 0:  # equal
            for ch in text:
                rpr = rprmap[orig_pos] if orig_pos < len(rprmap) else (rprmap[-1] if rprmap else None)
                segments.append(("eq", ch, rpr))
                orig_pos += 1
        elif op == -1:  # delete
            for ch in text:
                rpr = rprmap[orig_pos] if orig_pos < len(rprmap) else (rprmap[-1] if rprmap else None)
                segments.append(("del", ch, rpr))
                orig_pos += 1
        else:  # insert
            # use rPr of preceding original char, else first run rPr
            rpr = rprmap[orig_pos - 1] if orig_pos > 0 and rprmap else (rprmap[0] if rprmap else None)
            for ch in text:
                segments.append(("ins", ch, rpr))

    # Group consecutive segments with same (op, rpr_key)
    grouped = []
    for op, ch, rpr in segments:
        key = (op, rpr_key(rpr))
        if grouped and grouped[-1][0] == key:
            grouped[-1][1] += ch
        else:
            grouped.append([key, ch, rpr])

    for (op, _rk), text, rpr in grouped:
        if not text:
            continue
        if op == "eq":
            new_p.append(make_run(text, rpr))
        elif op == "ins":
            new_p.append(make_ins(text, rpr))
        elif op == "del":
            new_p.append(make_del(text, rpr))

    return new_p


def make_inserted_paragraph(text, pPr_template, rpr_template):
    """Fully-inserted paragraph: paragraph mark + run marked as inserted."""
    new_p = etree.Element(f"{WF}p")
    pPr = copy.deepcopy(pPr_template)
    # mark paragraph mark as inserted
    rPr_in_pPr = pPr.find(f"{WF}rPr")
    if rPr_in_pPr is None:
        rPr_in_pPr = etree.SubElement(pPr, f"{WF}rPr")
    ins_mark = etree.SubElement(rPr_in_pPr, f"{WF}ins")
    ins_mark.set(f"{WF}id", str(next_id()))
    ins_mark.set(f"{WF}author", AUTHOR)
    ins_mark.set(f"{WF}date", WHEN)
    new_p.append(pPr)
    # inserted run
    ins = etree.Element(f"{WF}ins")
    ins.set(f"{WF}id", str(next_id()))
    ins.set(f"{WF}author", AUTHOR)
    ins.set(f"{WF}date", WHEN)
    ins.append(make_run(text, rpr_template))
    new_p.append(ins)
    return new_p


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def main():
    with zipfile.ZipFile(ORIG) as z:
        names = z.namelist()
        doc_xml = z.read("word/document.xml")

    root = etree.fromstring(doc_xml)
    body = root.find(f"{WF}body")
    children = list(body)
    # children: 134 paragraphs (index 0..133) + sectPr (index 134)
    paragraphs = [c for c in children if c.tag == f"{WF}p"]
    sectPr = body.find(f"{WF}sectPr")

    # Template pPr / rPr
    pPr_section = paragraphs[21].find(f"{WF}pPr")
    pPr_body = paragraphs[22].find(f"{WF}pPr")
    pPr_list = paragraphs[43].find(f"{WF}pPr")
    rpr_section = paragraphs[21].findall(f"{WF}r")[0].find(f"{WF}rPr")
    rpr_body = paragraphs[22].findall(f"{WF}r")[0].find(f"{WF}rPr")

    # Build insertion map: after_index -> list of (text, kind)
    ins_map = {}
    for after_idx, items in INSERTIONS:
        ins_map.setdefault(after_idx, []).extend(items)

    # Rebuild body
    new_children = []
    for idx, p in enumerate(paragraphs):
        if idx in REPLACEMENTS:
            new_children.append(diff_paragraph(p, REPLACEMENTS[idx]))
        else:
            new_children.append(p)  # verbatim, full formatting preserved
        if idx in ins_map:
            for text, kind in ins_map[idx]:
                if kind == "section":
                    new_children.append(make_inserted_paragraph(text, pPr_section, rpr_section))
                elif kind == "list":
                    new_children.append(make_inserted_paragraph(text, pPr_list, rpr_body))
                else:
                    new_children.append(make_inserted_paragraph(text, pPr_body, rpr_body))

    # Clear body and re-append
    for c in list(body):
        body.remove(c)
    for c in new_children:
        body.append(c)
    if sectPr is not None:
        body.append(sectPr)

    new_doc_xml = etree.tostring(root, xml_declaration=True, encoding="UTF-8", standalone=True)

    OUT.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    with zipfile.ZipFile(ORIG) as zin, zipfile.ZipFile(OUT, "w", zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as zout:
        for item in zin.infolist():
            if item.filename == "word/document.xml":
                zout.writestr(item, new_doc_xml)
            else:
                zout.writestr(item, zin.read(item.filename))

    print(f"OK: wrote {OUT}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()"}

            t=child.find(f'{W}t')
            if t is not None and t.text:
                out.append(t.text)
    return ''.join(out)

{"content": "---
title: "PRIVILEGED AND CONFIDENTIAL — ATTORNEY-CLIENT COMMUNICATION / ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT"
---

# MEMORANDUM

| | |
|---|---|
| **TO:** | Tyler Huang, General Counsel, Greenleaf Organic Foods, Inc. |
| **FROM:** | Outside Employment Counsel |
| **DATE:** | May 12, 2025 |
| **RE:** | Review of Draft Settlement Agreement and General Release of All Claims (Delano v. Greenleaf) — Issues, Risks, and Redlined Revisions |
| **PRIVILEGE:** | Privileged & Confidential — Attorney-Client Communication / Attorney Work Product. Prepared at the request of, and for the benefit of, Greenleaf Organic Foods, Inc. |

---

## I. Executive Summary

You asked us to review the draft Settlement Agreement and General Release of All Claims dated May 5, 2025 (the "Draft"), prepared by Engel & Associates, P.C. on behalf of Marcus R. Delano, against Greenleaf's employment records and the company policies and plan documents referenced in the Draft. A redlined markup reflecting our recommended revisions accompanies this memorandum as **`settlement-agreement-markup.docx`**.

The Draft is a $525,000 settlement of Mr. Delano's retaliation, whistleblower, and age-discrimination claims arising from his November 8, 2024 internal cold-chain complaint, his December 3, 2024 Oregon OSHA complaint (No. OR-OSHA-2024-11872), and his March 14, 2025 termination. We identified **twenty-five (25) distinct issues**, ranging from defects that would render the age-discrimination release invalid to a material misstatement in the equity valuation to a significant piece of pretext evidence in Greenleaf's own records.

The most important conclusions are:

1. **The ADEA/age-discrimination release is likely invalid as drafted.** Mr. Delano is 58 years old (DOB 07/14/1966) and is protected by the Older Workers Benefit Protection Act ("OWBPA"). The Draft gives him only **14 days** to consider the agreement (Sections 5(c), 16) and is **entirely silent on the required 7-day revocation period**. OWBPA requires at least **21 days** to consider and a **7-day revocation period**. As written, Mr. Delano could sign the agreement and still sue Greenleaf for age discrimination. This is the single most important defect and has been redlined.

2. **The RSU valuation in the Draft is wrong and contains a false statement.** Section 3(d) values 5,000 accelerated RSUs at **$10.00/share ($50,000)**, stating this is "based on the most recent independent valuation conducted by Ridgepoint Valuation Services." Greenleaf's equity records show the most recent 409A valuation is dated **January 15, 2025 at $12.50/share**. The correct value is **$62,500**, and the correct total settlement value is **$537,500, not $525,000** — a **$12,500 understatement**. The Draft's statement that $10.00 is the "most recent" valuation is false. This has been redlined.

3. **Greenleaf's own records contain powerful pretext evidence.** The employment summary shows that **Raj Mehta was hired as Senior Director of Supply Chain & Logistics on March 20, 2025 — six days after Mr. Delano's March 14, 2025 termination — at $185,000.** The termination was cited as "elimination of position due to restructuring." Hiring a replacement within days directly contradicts the stated rationale and substantially strengthens Mr. Delano's retaliation and age-discrimination claims. This strongly supports the decision to settle, but it also makes the non-disparagement and reference provisions (which encode the "restructuring" narrative) fragile.

4. **The Draft is missing several provisions essential to protect Greenleaf**, including any governing-law/venue/dispute-resolution clause, a return-of-property clause, a confidential-information/trade-secret clause, a cooperation clause, and any address of Greenleaf's EPLI insurance. These have been added in the redline.

5. **Several provisions are unenforceable or unlawful as drafted**, including the non-compete's coverage of **California** (void under Cal. Bus. & Prof. Code § 16600) and the confidentiality/non-disparagement clauses' failure to carve out the right to file with or cooperate with government agencies (EEOC, OSHA, SEC, NLRB, Oregon BOLI).

We recommend that Greenleaf **not execute the Draft in its current form**. The accompanying redline addresses the defects we identified; the items below that require factual follow-up (the 2024 bonus, EPLI consent-to-settle, Compensation Committee approval) should be resolved before execution.

---

## II. Documents Reviewed

1. **Draft Settlement Agreement and General Release of All Claims**, dated May 5, 2025, prepared by Engel & Associates, P.C. (the "Draft").
2. **Greenleaf Employment Summary for Marcus R. Delano** (Employee ID GRN-2017-0438), prepared by Leanne Foss, HR Director, dated May 10, 2025, containing: (a) Employment History; (b) Compensation History (2017–2024); and (c) Equity Details, including the RSU Grant Summary, Vesting Schedule, 409A Valuation History, Status at Termination, and Settlement Proposal Analysis (the "Employment Records").
3. **Company policies and plan documents referenced in the Draft**: the Greenleaf 2021 Equity Incentive Plan and the RSU Grant Agreement dated July 1, 2022 (as documented in the Equity Details tab of the Employment Records).

---

## III. Summary of Redline Revisions

The redline (`settlement-agreement-markup.docx`) contains tracked insertions and deletions. The principal revisions are:

| # | Location | Revision |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | §1(b), Preamble | Effective Date redefined as the 8th day after the last signature, following the 7-day revocation period (no longer fixed at May 5, 2025). |
| 2 | §2 | Qualified the "all wages/compensation received" representation to acknowledge the back-pay payment includes any unpaid 2024 incentive comp and PTO; removed "not in dispute" language. |
| 3 | §3(b) | Clarified back pay includes unpaid 2024 performance incentive compensation. |
| 4 | §3(d) | Corrected RSU value to $12.50/share (Jan. 15, 2025 409A) = $62,500; added Compensation Committee approval as a condition precedent with a $62,500 cash fallback. |
| 5 | §3(e) | Corrected total settlement value to $537,500. |
| 6 | §4 (new ¶) | Added government-agency / whistleblower carve-out (right to file, cooperate, and receive awards; OSHA investigation unaffected). |
| 7 | §5(c) | Changed consideration period from 14 to 21 days. |
| 8 | §5(g) (new) | Added 7-day revocation period (OWBPA). |
| 9 | §6(iv)–(v) | Added government-agency reporting carve-out to confidentiality. |
| 10 | §7 | Added government-agency/truthful-statement carve-out; changed perpetual duration to 3 years. |
| 11 | §9 | Removed California from non-compete; added ORS 653.295 acknowledgment and California carve-out. |
| 12 | §11 | Softened "positive" reference to "neutral, factual"; revised mandated praise. |
| 13 | §15(a)(ii) | Added government-agency carve-out to the no-future-charges representation. |
| 14 | §16 | Changed consideration period to 21 days; added revocation-period mechanics. |
| 15 | §18 | Carved out the Equity Plan, RSU Grant, and prior confidentiality/restrictive-covenant agreements from the supersession clause. |
| 16 | §19 (new) | Return of Company Property (with 401(k) information). |
| 17 | §20 (new) | Confidential Information and Trade Secrets (with DTSA immunity notice). |
| 18 | §21 (new) | Cooperation. |
| 19 | §22 (new) | Insurance (EPLI cooperation; payments unconditional). |
| 20 | §23 (new) | Governing Law, Venue, and Jury Waiver. |

---

## IV. Issues and Risks

Each issue below is rated **Critical**, **High**, **Medium**, or **Lower** based on its potential effect on the enforceability of the release, Greenleaf's financial exposure, or Greenleaf's litigation risk.

### Issue 1 — ADEA/OWBPA release is likely invalid: 14-day consideration period and no revocation period. [Critical]

**The problem.** Mr. Delano is 58 years old (DOB 07/14/1966; age 58 as of the report date of May 10, 2025). His age-discrimination claim under the Age Discrimination in Employment Act ("ADEA") is the core of the settlement. For a release of ADEA claims to be "knowing and voluntary" under the Older Workers Benefit Protection Act ("OWBPA"), 29 U.S.C. § 626(f), the agreement must (a) give the individual **at least 21 days** to consider the agreement (45 days only if the program is part of a group exit-incentive or other termination program — this is an individual settlement, so 21 days applies), and (b) allow **at least 7 days to revoke** after signing, during which the agreement is not effective or enforceable.

The Draft provides only **fourteen (14) calendar days** to consider (Sections 5(c) and 16) and is **entirely silent on any revocation period**. Neither requirement is satisfied.

**Consequence.** The ADEA release is almost certainly invalid. Mr. Delano could execute the agreement, accept the $525,000, and thereafter file an ADEA charge and lawsuit, arguing the waiver was not knowing and voluntary. Greenleaf would have paid the consideration and received no enforceable release of the age claim — the most valuable claim in the settlement. This is the single greatest risk in the Draft.

**Redline / recommendation.** Section 5(c) and Section 16 revised to **21 days**; new Section 5(g) adds the **7-day revocation period** with revocation mechanics; the Effective Date (Section 1(b)) is redefined as the eighth day after the last signature (i.e., after the revocation period expires without revocation). These changes protect Greenleaf by making the ADEA release enforceable.

### Issue 2 — RSU valuation uses an outdated 409A valuation and contains a false statement; total settlement value understated by $12,500. [Critical]

**The problem.** Section 3(d) values the 5,000 accelerated RSUs at **Ten Dollars ($10.00) per share**, stating this is "based on the most recent independent valuation conducted by Ridgepoint Valuation Services," yielding a total RSU value of **$50,000**. Section 3(e) aggregates the total settlement value at **$525,000**.

Greenleaf's Equity Details tell a different story. The 409A Valuation History (Sub-Table C) shows four valuations by Ridgepoint:

| Valuation Date | FMV/Share | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| July 1, 2022 | $8.00 | At time of RSU grant |
| July 1, 2023 | $9.25 | Annual valuation |
| July 1, 2024 | $10.00 | Annual valuation |
| **January 15, 2025** | **$12.50** | **Most recent 409A valuation — CURRENT** |

The **January 15, 2025** valuation at **$12.50/share** is the most recent. The Draft's statement that $10.00 is "based on the most recent independent valuation" is **false**. The Settlement Proposal Analysis (Sub-Table E) in Greenleaf's own records expressly flags this: "Draft settlement agreement uses July 2024 409A valuation ($10.00/share). The most recent 409A valuation performed by Ridgepoint Valuation Services dated January 15, 2025 values shares at $12.50/share. The correct value of 5,000 accelerated RSUs is $62,500, not $50,000. The corrected total settlement value should be $537,500 instead of $525,000."

**Consequence.** (i) The agreement contains a material misstatement of fact that could support a fraudulent-inducement or mutual-mistake challenge to the RSU provision (and, by extension, the release). (ii) The total consideration is understated by **$12,500** ($62,500 − $50,000), and the total settlement value should be **$537,500**, not $525,000. (iii) Using a stale 409A value for a settlement may also create tax-reporting and 409A-compliance concerns.

**Redline / recommendation.** Section 3(d) corrected to **$12.50/share**, expressly referencing the **January 15, 2025** 409A valuation, with a total RSU value of **$62,500**; Section 3(e) corrected to **$537,500**. Confirm with the plan administrator and tax counsel that the corrected value and reporting are consistent with the 2021 Equity Incentive Plan and 409A.

### Issue 3 — RSU acceleration is unconditional; the plan requires Compensation Committee approval. [High]

**The problem.** Section 3(d) states that "Greenleaf shall cause the immediate accelerated vesting" of 5,000 RSUs, with no condition. The Equity Details (Sub-Table A) state that the "Compensation Committee has discretion to approve accelerated vesting in connection with separation agreements" under the 2021 Equity Incentive Plan, and the Forfeiture Provision provides that unvested RSUs are forfeited upon termination unless an exception (death, disability, Change of Control) applies — none of which is present here. Acceleration is therefore not automatic; it requires Compensation Committee action.

**Consequence.** If the Compensation Committee declines to approve the acceleration, Greenleaf cannot deliver the shares and would be in breach of the agreement as drafted, exposing Greenleaf to a breach-of-contract claim (and potentially reviving the released claims if the breach is material). The Draft gives Greenleaf no protection.

**Redline / recommendation.** Section 3(d) revised to make accelerated vesting **subject to Compensation Committee approval** as an express condition precedent, with Greenleaf's obligation to seek approval promptly, and a **cash fallback of $62,500** (at Mr. Delano's election) if approval is not obtained within 30 days. **Action item:** obtain Compensation Committee approval (or document the process) before or promptly after execution.

### Issue 4 — Greenleaf's records contain strong pretext evidence: a replacement was hired six days after termination. [Critical — litigation risk]

**The problem.** The Employment History states that Mr. Delano was terminated on **March 14, 2025**, with the stated reason "Elimination of position due to restructuring of supply chain division." The same record states: "**Replacement Hire: Raj Mehta, Senior Director of Supply Chain & Logistics, hired March 20, 2025, salary $185,000.**" A replacement was therefore hired **six days** after the termination, in the same functional area (supply chain and logistics), at a salary ($185,000) below Mr. Delano's $218,000 base.

**Consequence.** This is powerful evidence that the "elimination of position" rationale is pretextual. It directly supports Mr. Delano's retaliation claim under ORS 654.062(5) (he complained internally on November 8, 2024 and to Oregon OSHA on December 3, 2024, and was placed on leave February 10, 2025 and terminated March 14, 2025) and his ADEA claim (he was replaced by a lower-paid individual; the timing is suspect). The close temporal proximity between protected activity and adverse action, combined with a near-immediate replacement, makes this a strong plaintiff case. Two practical implications:

- **The settlement is well-advised.** Greenleaf's litigation exposure (back pay, front pay, compensatory and (under ORS 659A) punitive damages, attorney fees, and Oregon OSHA penalties) likely exceeds the $537,500 corrected value. Settling is the right call.
- **The "restructuring" narrative is fragile.** Sections 7 (non-disparagement) and 11 (reference) encode the "restructuring/elimination of position" story. If the Raj Mehta hire surfaces — through the pending Oregon OSHA investigation, public records, LinkedIn, or a prospective-employer reference check — the narrative collapses and Greenleaf's good-faith position is undermined. Greenleaf should ensure the release is airtight and should not rely on the non-disparagement/reference provisions to suppress facts that are already in the company's own records.

**Redline / recommendation.** No redline can redact this evidence. We recommend: (i) confirm the release covers all claims arising from the replacement hire and the termination rationale; (ii) preserve litigation hold / document-retention for the OSHA investigation; (iii) consider whether the reference provision (Section 11) should avoid affirmatively stating a "reorganization" reason that the records contradict (the redline softens the reference to "neutral, factual" and states the position "was eliminated as part of a reorganization" — Greenleaf should satisfy itself that this statement is defensible, or further limit the reference to dates and title only).

### Issue 5 — No governing law, venue, or dispute-resolution provision. [High]

**The problem.** The Draft contains no choice-of-law clause, no forum-selection/venue clause, no jury waiver, and no arbitration clause. Disputes about enforcement (e.g., alleged breach of the non-compete, confidentiality, or non-disparagement provisions) could therefore be brought in an uncertain forum under uncertain law.

**Consequence.** Greenleaf loses predictability and home-court advantage, and may face a jury in an employment-friendly forum on a breach claim. This is a significant gap for a company that is imposing post-termination restrictive covenants.

**Redline / recommendation.** New Section 23 added: **Oregon law** governs; exclusive venue in **Multnomah County, Oregon** state court or the **U.S. District Court for the District of Oregon**; mutual **jury-trial waiver**; and fee-shifting to the prevailing party. (If Greenleaf prefers arbitration, we can substitute an arbitration clause; confirm preference.)

### Issue 6 — Non-compete covers California, where non-competes are void. [High]

**The problem.** Section 9 restricts Mr. Delano from working for an organic-food competitor "within the states of Oregon, Washington, Idaho, **and California**."
